brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Yes) via valid Bayesian inference with correct arithmetic; traces second-order causal suppression logic explaining *why* lirg is evidence against jyka, going well beyond surface-level; proceeds linearly without naming potential biases or flagging the uniform-prior assumption as a choice; re-derives P(lirg) as a normalizing constant and confirms posterior exceeds prior, constituting basic verification; assumes uniform prior P(jyka)=0.5 without flagging it as an assumption or noting the conclusion would hold for any prior (since the likelihood ratio favors ¬jyka regardless); never considers alternative priors, alternative causal interpretations, or what the presence of unobserved Gyzp might complicate; every derivation step is shown in full — causal graph, given values, Bayes formula, arithmetic, and conclusion are all explicit and reproducible.
